executive_summary: Canada is experiencing an unprecedented ransomware surge with over 100 organizations compromised in a single 48-hour period. High-profile victims include Canada Goose (hit by two separate groups), Markham Stouffville Hospital, National Money Mart (database leaked), Eastern Townships School Board, and Peterborough Public Health. The Qilin ransomware group leads with 19 victims, followed by SafePay (14), Play (10), and Clop (13). Critical infrastructure sectors affected include healthcare, education, financial services, and municipal government.

canada_landscape.summary: Canada experienced a catastrophic ransomware wave affecting 100+ organizations across all provinces in 48 hours. Critical sectors hit include healthcare (Markham Stouffville Hospital, Labtician Ophthalmics, BioPharma Services), education (Eastern Townships School Board, Collge Superieur De Montreal, teccart.qc.ca), public sector (Peterborough Public Health, Municipality of North Perth, Workers Health & Safety Centre, Thunder Bay Counselling), and municipal government. No CCCS advisories were issued during this period despite the scale of attacks.
canada_landscape.retailers: Canada Goose compromised by two separate ransomware groups (CoinbaseCartel and ShinyHunters). Active Green + Ross (automotive retail chain) and Kids & Company (childcare services) also hit.
canada_landscape.financial: National Money Mart Company suffered a database leak via Everest ransomware. Morison Insurance Brokers compromised by Sinobi group. Multiple accounting firms hit including Pewarchuk CPA, MG Chartered Professional Accountant. SMT Lawyers (SMTLAWYERS.CA) also targeted.

report_2w.executive_summary: Adobe Acrobat and Reader users face immediate risk from CVE-2026-34621, a zero-day vulnerability exploited since December 2025. CISA has added six actively exploited flaws affecting Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including a 14-year-old Microsoft flaw. Major breaches hit Booking.com (customer data exposed) and potentially Rockstar Games (GTA 6 developer), with ShinyHunters claiming attacks on Amtrak (9.4M Salesforce records) and Anodot. A critical supply chain attack targeted the Axios JavaScript library (CVSS 10.0), enabling full cloud compromise via prototype pollution. Canadian cybersecurity authorities issued an alert for Fortinet FortiClientEMS (CVE-2026-35616).

items.description: Known exploited vulnerability in Fortinet, Microsoft, or Adobe software
Malicious Axios supply chain incident affecting NPM ecosystem

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian Centre for Cyber Security (CCCS) issued Alert AL26-007 for CVE-2026-35616 affecting Fortinet FortiClientEMS on April 7, 2026. No direct attacks on Canadian critical infrastructure were reported during this period. Canadian political news dominated Mastodon feeds with Prime Minister Mark Carney's Liberal Party securing a majority government.
canada_landscape.retailers: CBC investigation found Loblaws and Sobeys overcharging customers for underweight meat products (repeat violation). No cybersecurity incidents reported for major Canadian retailers (Canadian Tire, Shoppers Drug Mart, SportChek).
